# `getElementId`

Returns the `id` of a specified DOM element. If the element exists but does not have an `id` attribute, the function will automatically generate a unique identifier, assign it to the element, and return it.

Additionally, the function allows appending a CSS selector to the returned string, so you can immediately use the result as a compound selector (e.g., `"#id-123456 .child-class"`).

**Parameters:**
- `element?: ElementOrString<E>` — A DOM element or selector from which to obtain the ID.
- `selector?: string` — An optional string that will be appended to the returned ID (like pseudo-classes or descendant selectors).

**Returns:**
`string` — The element's ID string, or a newly generated ID string if the element is not found.

```typescript
import { getElementId } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Element has no ID
const div = document.createElement('div')
console.log(div.id) // ''

const id = getElementId(div)
console.log(id) // For example: 'id-123456'
console.log(div.id) // 'id-123456' (assigned automatically)

// Using an additional selector
const selector = getElementId(div, ' > span.active')
console.log(selector) // '#id-123456 > span.active'
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getElementImage - Get image element

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getelementimage-get-image-element--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getElementImage/getElementImage.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getElementImage - Get image element'/>

# `getElementImage`

Used to safely retrieve an `HTMLImageElement` node. If a string (an image URL) is passed as input, the function automatically creates an `<img>` tag and assigns the URL to its `src` attribute. If an `HTMLImageElement` object is already passed, it simply returns it unchanged.

**Parameters:**
- `image: HTMLImageElement | string` — An image element or a string source (URL).

**Returns:**
`HTMLImageElement | undefined` — A ready-to-use `<img>` tag, or `undefined` if the environment does not allow its creation (e.g., no DOM).

```typescript
import { getElementImage } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Passing a string URL
const img1 = getElementImage('/path/to/image.png')
console.log(img1 instanceof HTMLImageElement) // true
console.log(img1?.src) // 'http://.../path/to/image.png'

// Passing an existing img tag
const existingImg = document.createElement('img')
const img2 = getElementImage(existingImg)
console.log(img1 === existingImg) // true
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getElementItem - Retrieve element property value

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getelementitem-retrieve-element-property-value--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getElementItem/getElementItem.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getElementItem - Retrieve element property value'/>

# `getElementItem`

Returns the value of a specific property from an HTML element (e.g., `id`, `className`, or any custom property if it's defined as a key on the element type). The utility uses `getElement` to find the node, and if the element is not found or lacks the specified property, it returns `defaultValue` (which defaults to `undefined`).

**Parameters:**
- `element: ElementOrString<T>` — A selector string or an existing DOM element.
- `index: K | string` — The property name (key) of the element whose value is required.
- `defaultValue?: D` — A fallback value returned if the property is not found.

**Returns:**
`T[K] | D | undefined` — The value of the requested property or the `defaultValue`.

```typescript
import { getElementItem } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const input = document.createElement('input')
input.type = 'text'
input.id = 'my-input'

// Getting the ID
const elementId = getElementItem(input, 'id') 
console.log(elementId) // 'my-input'

// If the property is missing and we provide a fallback
const testValue = getElementItem('.non-existent', 'id', 'fallback-id')
console.log(testValue) // 'fallback-id'
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getElementOrWindow - Retrieve Element or Window

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getelementorwindow-retrieve-element-or-window--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getElementOrWindow/getElementOrWindow.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getElementOrWindow - Retrieve Element or Window'/>

# `getElementOrWindow`

This utility is similar to `getElement`, but with one significant difference: if the global `window` object is passed as an argument, the function returns it exactly. `getElement` in the same scenario would return `document.body`.

If a string or another element is passed, the function behaves exactly like the standard `getElement` utility.

**Parameters:**
- `element?: ElementOrString<E>` — A selector to search for, an `Element` node, or the `Window` object.

**Returns:**
`E | undefined` — The located element, the originally passed element, the `window` object (if it was passed), or `undefined` if nothing matched.

```typescript
import { getElementOrWindow } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Passing Window returns Window (by contrast, getElement would return document.body)
const win = getElementOrWindow(window)

// Normal search by selector
const btn = getElementOrWindow('.submit-btn')
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getExactSearchExp - Build a RegExp for exact match

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getexactsearchexp-build-a-regexp-for-exact-match--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getExactSearchExp/getExactSearchExp.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getExactSearchExp - Build a RegExp for exact match'/>

# `getExactSearchExp`

A utility that builds a case-insensitive `RegExp` for an exact match of the given string.

Unlike `getSearchExp`, this function matches the entire string (with special characters escaped) without splitting it into separate words.

**Parameters:**
- `search: string` — The search string for exact matching.

**Returns:**
`RegExp` — A regular expression with the `i` flag (case-insensitive).

```typescript
import { getExactSearchExp } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const regex = getExactSearchExp('hello')

regex.test('hello')       // true
regex.test('HELLO')       // true
regex.test('hello world') // false — not an exact match
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getExp - Create a RegExp with escaped string

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getexp-create-a-regexp-with-escaped-string--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getExp/getExp.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getExp - Create a RegExp with escaped string'/>

# `getExp`

A utility that creates a `RegExp` object from a template string by first escaping all special regular expression characters within it (such as `.` `*` `+` `?` `^` `$` `{` `}` `(` `)` `|` `[` `]` `\`).

This is essential when the search string comes from user input or contains characters that could break a regular expression. The function allows you to specify the regex flags and a pattern string where the escaped text will replace the `:value` token.

**Parameters:**
- `value: string` — The target string to be escaped and matched.
- `flags?: string` — (optional) Regular expression flags (e.g., `'ig'`). Defaults to `'ig'` (case-insensitive and global search).
- `pattern?: string` — (optional) The regular expression template string. Defaults to just `':value'`.

**Returns:**
`RegExp` — The constructed regular expression object.

```typescript
import { getExp } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Basic search
const rx1 = getExp('user.name')
console.log(rx1) // /user\.name/gi  (dot is escaped)

// Search with a custom flag and pattern (e.g., "starts with")
const rx2 = getExp('query[1]', 'i', '^:value.*')
console.log(rx2) // /^query\[1\].*/i
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getItemByPath - Access deeply nested properties

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getitembypath-access-deeply-nested-properties--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getItemByPath/getItemByPath.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getItemByPath - Access deeply nested properties'/>

# `getItemByPath`

A utility that safely retrieves a value from a deeply nested object using a path string with dot (`.`) separated keys. If the property or any intermediate segment is not found, the function gracefully returns `undefined` (preventing *Cannot read properties of undefined* errors).

**Note:** The function allows specifying a generic return type `R` (which defaults to `string`). It supports accessing array items by using a stringified index in the path (e.g., `"list.0.name"`).

**Parameters:**
- `item: T` — The target object or array to inspect.
- `path: string` — The dot-separated property path (e.g., `"user.address.city"` or `"list.1.id"`).

**Returns:**
`R | undefined` — The value of the requested property (of type `R`), or `undefined` if any segment in the path is missing.

```typescript
import { getItemByPath } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const data = {
  profile: {
    details: {
      bio: 'UI Developer'
    }
  },
  roles: ['admin', 'manager']
}

// Successful retrieval
console.log(getItemByPath(data, 'profile.details.bio')) // 'UI Developer'

// Accessing an array via a string index
console.log(getItemByPath(data, 'roles.0')) // 'admin'

// Safely failing on nonexistent paths
console.log(getItemByPath(data, 'profile.missing.key')) // undefined
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getKey - Get pressed key code

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getkey-get-pressed-key-code--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getKey/getKey.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getKey - Get pressed key code'/>

# `getKey`

Returns the code or name of the key that was pressed, based on the `KeyboardEvent` object. This utility normalizes retrieving the key identifier by safely attempting properties in the following priority order: `event.key` → `event.code` → stringified `event.keyCode`. If all fail, it returns `undefined`.

**Parameters:**
- `event: KeyboardEvent` — The keyboard event object (triggered by `keydown`, `keyup`, or `keypress`).

**Returns:**
`string` — The name or code of the pressed key (e.g., `'Enter'`, `'Escape'`, `'A'`).

```typescript
import { getKey } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  const keyName = getKey(e)
  
  if (keyName === 'Escape') {
    console.log('User pressed Escape! Closing menu.')
  }
})
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getLengthOfAllArray - Lengths of all array elements

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getlengthofallarray-lengths-of-all-array-elements--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getLengthOfAllArray/getLengthOfAllArray.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getLengthOfAllArray - Lengths of all array elements'/>

# `getLengthOfAllArray`

Creates and returns a new array consisting of the lengths (the `.length` property) of all elements in the provided array of strings.

**Parameters:**
- `value: ObjectOrArray<string>` — The input array of strings. If a single string is passed, it acts as an array with one element.

**Returns:**
`number[]` — An array of numbers where each number corresponds to the length of the string in the source array at that same position.

```typescript
import { getLengthOfAllArray } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const data = ['red', 'blue', 'green']
const lengths = getLengthOfAllArray(data)

console.log(lengths) // [3, 4, 5]
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMaxLengthAllArray - Maximum length in array

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getmaxlengthallarray-maximum-length-in-array--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getMaxLengthAllArray/getMaxLengthAllArray.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMaxLengthAllArray - Maximum length in array'/>

# `getMaxLengthAllArray`

A utility that finds the longest string in the provided array and returns its length (as a number). The function uses built-in `Math.max` combined with `getLengthOfAllArray`.

**Parameters:**
- `data: ObjectOrArray<string>` — The input array of strings.

**Returns:**
`number` — The length of the longest string in the array. If an empty array is passed, it returns `-Infinity`.

```typescript
import { getMaxLengthAllArray } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const phrases = ['Hello', 'Good morning', 'Greetings']
const maxLength = getMaxLengthAllArray(phrases)

console.log(maxLength) // 12 ("Good morning")
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMinLengthAllArray - Minimum length in array

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getminlengthallarray-minimum-length-in-array--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getMinLengthAllArray/getMinLengthAllArray.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMinLengthAllArray - Minimum length in array'/>

# `getMinLengthAllArray`

A utility that finds the shortest string in the provided array and returns its length (as a number). The function uses built-in `Math.min` combined with `getLengthOfAllArray`.

**Parameters:**
- `data: ObjectOrArray<string>` — The input array of strings.

**Returns:**
`number` — The length of the shortest string in the array. If an empty array is passed, it returns `Infinity`.

```typescript
import { getMinLengthAllArray } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const words = ['computer', 'code', 'programming']
const minLength = getMinLengthAllArray(words)

console.log(minLength) // 4 ("code")
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMouseClient - Get X and Y coordinates of an event

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getmouseclient-get-x-and-y-coordinates-of-an-event--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getMouseClient/getMouseClient.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMouseClient - Get X and Y coordinates of an event'/>

# `getMouseClient`

Extracts the X and Y coordinates of the pointer (mouse or touch) relative to the visible area of the browser window (viewport) from the event object.

The function handles both standard mouse events (`MouseEvent`) and touch events (`TouchEvent`), providing a unified interface (returning an `ImageCoordinator` object containing `x` and `y` properties). This is especially useful when creating drag-and-drop interfaces, sliders, and other interactive elements meant to work across different devices.

**Parameters:**
- `event: MouseEvent & TouchEvent` — The event object (`mousemove`, `touchmove`, `click`, etc.).

**Returns:**
`ImageCoordinator` — An object containing `x` and `y` coordinate properties. If coordinates are not found in the event, it defaults to `{ x: 0, y: 0 }`.

```typescript
import { getMouseClient } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

document.addEventListener('mousemove', (event) => {
  const coords = getMouseClient(event)
  console.log(`Cursor is currently at X: ${coords.x}, Y: ${coords.y}`)
})

document.addEventListener('touchmove', (event) => {
  const coords = getMouseClient(event)
  console.log(`Touch is currently at X: ${coords.x}, Y: ${coords.y}`)
})
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMouseClientX - Get X coordinate of an event

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getmouseclientx-get-x-coordinate-of-an-event--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getMouseClientX/getMouseClientX.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMouseClientX - Get X coordinate of an event'/>

# `getMouseClientX`

Extracts the X coordinate (horizontal position) of the pointer (mouse or touch) relative to the visible area of the browser window (viewport) from the event object.

The function processes both standard mouse events (`MouseEvent`) and touch events (`TouchEvent`), giving priority to the `clientX` property. If `clientX` is not found directly on the event, it searches within the `targetTouches` and `touches` arrays.

**Parameters:**
- `event: MouseEvent & TouchEvent` — The event object (`mousemove`, `touchmove`, `click`, etc.).

**Returns:**
`number` — The X coordinate of the cursor/touch. If not found, it returns `0`.

```typescript
import { getMouseClientX } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

document.addEventListener('click', (event) => {
  const x = getMouseClientX(event)
  console.log(`The user clicked ${x}px away from the left edge of the window`)
})
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMouseClientY - Get Y coordinate of an event

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getmouseclienty-get-y-coordinate-of-an-event--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getMouseClientY/getMouseClientY.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getMouseClientY - Get Y coordinate of an event'/>

# `getMouseClientY`

Extracts the Y coordinate (vertical position) of the pointer (mouse or touch) relative to the visible area of the browser window (viewport) from the event object.

The function processes both standard mouse events (`MouseEvent`) and touch events (`TouchEvent`), giving priority to the `clientY` property. If `clientY` is not found directly on the event, it searches within the `targetTouches` and `touches` arrays.

**Parameters:**
- `event: MouseEvent & TouchEvent` — The event object (`mousemove`, `touchmove`, `click`, etc.).

**Returns:**
`number` — The Y coordinate of the cursor/touch. If not found, it returns `0`.

```typescript
import { getMouseClientY } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

document.addEventListener('click', (event) => {
  const y = getMouseClientY(event)
  console.log(`The user clicked ${y}px away from the top edge of the window`)
})
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getObjectByKeys - Object by key list

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getobjectbykeys-object-by-key-list--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getObjectByKeys/getObjectByKeys.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getObjectByKeys - Object by key list'/>

# `getObjectByKeys`

Creates and returns a new object containing only the properties corresponding to the specified keys from the source object. Properties that do not exist or have a value strictly equal to `undefined` are ignored during copying.

**Parameters:**
- `data: Record<string, any>` — The source object containing the initial data.
- `keys: string[]` — An array of string keys to extract from the source.

**Returns:**
`Record<string, any>` — A new object containing only the successfully extracted properties. If a property is `undefined` in the source, it will not be included in the result.

```typescript
import { getObjectByKeys } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const user = {
  id: 1,
  name: 'Ivan',
  role: 'admin',
  age: undefined, // undefined values are skipped
  active: true
}

const result = getObjectByKeys(user, ['id', 'name', 'age', 'token'])
console.log(result) // { id: 1, name: 'Ivan' }
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getObjectNoUndefined - Remove properties with an exception value

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getobjectnoundefined-remove-properties-with-an-exception-value--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getObjectNoUndefined/getObjectNoUndefined.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getObjectNoUndefined - Remove properties with an exception value'/>

# `getObjectNoUndefined`

Creates and returns a new object containing only the properties from the source object or array whose values are **not strictly equal** to the exception value. By default, the exception value is `undefined`.

This function is useful for cleaning up data before sending it to a server or for object normalization. When an array is passed, its indices will be converted into string keys in the resulting object.

**Parameters:**
- `data: T` — The source object or array (where `T extends Record<string | number, any>`).
- `exception: any` — The value used as criteria for removal. Defaults to `undefined`.

**Returns:**
`T` — A new object devoid of properties containing the specified exception value.

```typescript
import { getObjectNoUndefined } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const user = {
  name: 'John',
  age: 25,
  description: undefined, // Will be removed automatically (by default)
  status: 'guest'
}

console.log(getObjectNoUndefined(user)) 
// { name: 'John', age: 25, status: 'guest' }

// Remove all properties mapped to the value 'guest'
console.log(getObjectNoUndefined(user, 'guest'))
// { name: 'John', age: 25, description: undefined }
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getObjectOrNone - Safe object return

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getobjectornone-safe-object-return--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getObjectOrNone/getObjectOrNone.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getObjectOrNone - Safe object return'/>

# `getObjectOrNone`

A simple utility that checks whether the passed value is an actual object (not `null`, not an array, not a primitive) and returns it. If the passed argument is not a plain object, a new empty object `{}` is returned. This function is useful for safe variable initialization or parameter assignments.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — Any value, usually expected to be an object.

**Returns:**
`T & Record<string, any>` — The original object if the check passes; otherwise, an empty object `{}`.

```typescript
import { getObjectOrNone } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Successful object return
console.log(getObjectOrNone({ name: 'Admin' })) // { name: 'Admin' }

// Arrays are not considered plain objects, returns {}
console.log(getObjectOrNone([1, 2, 3])) // {}

// Primitives and null return {}
console.log(getObjectOrNone(null)) // {}
console.log(getObjectOrNone('a string')) // {}
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getRandomText - Generate random text

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getrandomtext-generate-random-text--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getRandomText/getRandomText.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getRandomText - Generate random text'/>

# `getRandomText`

Generates a string consisting of a random number of "words". A word is defined as a sequence of identical symbols of a specific length. This function is useful for populating UI layouts with placeholder text that mimics the structure of real text without carrying any meaning or language constraints.

It relies on the `random` (for choosing random numbers) and `strFill` (for repeating the symbol) utility functions.

**Parameters:**
- `min: number` — Minimum number of words in the resulting string.
- `max: number` — Maximum number of words in the resulting string.
- `symbol: string` — (Optional) The character used to construct the generated words. Defaults to `#`.
- `lengthMin: number` — (Optional) Minimum length of a single word. Defaults to `2`.
- `lengthMax: number` — (Optional) Maximum length of a single word. Defaults to `12`.

**Returns:**
`string` — A string of space-separated generated words.

```typescript
import { getRandomText } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Generate between 2 and 5 words made of '#' symbols
// Length of each word ranges from 2 to 12 chars
console.log(getRandomText(2, 5)) // '#### ## #######'

// Exact number of words (3) with a specific symbol ('*')
// Word lengths are strictly 5 chars
console.log(getRandomText(3, 3, '*', 5, 5)) // '***** ***** *****'
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getRequestString - Build query string

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getrequeststring-build-query-string--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getRequestString/getRequestString.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getRequestString - Build query string'/>

# `getRequestString`

Transforms a dictionary object into a URL-style formatted string (by default, a query string for GET parameters) where keys and values are joined by designated delimiters. Before string construction, all values are cast to strings, trimmed of whitespace, and passed through `encodeURIComponent` to ensure safe URL transmission. Keys are sorted alphabetically.

**Parameters:**
- `request: Record<string, any>` — The data object to convert.
- `sign: string` — (Optional) The character linking a key to its value. Defaults to `=`.
- `separator: string` — (Optional) The character separating pairs. Defaults to `&`.

**Returns:**
`string` — A formatted and sorted string.

```typescript
import { getRequestString } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const params = {
  sort: 'desc',
  page: 2,
  search: 'comfortable chair'
}

console.log(getRequestString(params)) 
// 'page=2&search=comfortable%20chair&sort=desc'
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getSearchExp - Build a RegExp matching all search words

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getsearchexp-build-a-regexp-matching-all-search-words--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getSearchExp/getSearchExp.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getSearchExp - Build a RegExp matching all search words'/>

# `getSearchExp`

A utility that builds a case-insensitive global `RegExp` matching strings that contain **all** words from the search string (in any order).

Each word is split by spaces, escaped, and wrapped in a lookahead `(?=.*?word)`. This makes it ideal for live search or multi-word filtering UIs.

**Parameters:**
- `search: string` — A search string consisting of one or more space-separated words.

**Returns:**
`RegExp` — A regular expression with flags `ig` (global, case-insensitive).

```typescript
import { getSearchExp } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const regex = getSearchExp('foo bar')

regex.test('foo bar baz')  // true  — contains both words
regex.test('bar foo')      // true  — order doesn't matter
regex.test('foo only')     // false — word bar is missing

// Special characters are escaped automatically
const regex2 = getSearchExp('price $10.00')
regex2.test('price $10.00') // true
regex2.test('price X10Y00') // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getSeparatingSearchExp - Build a RegExp for space-separated word search

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getseparatingsearchexp-build-a-regexp-for-space-separated-word-search--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getSeparatingSearchExp/getSeparatingSearchExp.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getSeparatingSearchExp - Build a RegExp for space-separated word search'/>

# `getSeparatingSearchExp`

A utility that builds a case-insensitive global `RegExp` for searching strings containing **any** of the words from the search string (separated by spaces).

Each word is split by spaces, escaped, and joined with the OR operator `|`. This is useful when you want to find a match for at least one of the entered words.

**Parameters:**
- `search: string` — A search string consisting of one or more space-separated words.

**Returns:**
`RegExp` — A regular expression with flags `ig` (global, case-insensitive).

```typescript
import { getSeparatingSearchExp } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const regex = getSeparatingSearchExp('foo bar')

regex.test('foo')          // true
regex.test('bar')          // true
regex.test('foo bar baz')  // true
regex.test('something')    // false

// Special characters are escaped automatically
const regex2 = getSeparatingSearchExp('$10 .')
regex2.test('$10') // true
regex2.test('.')   // true
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getStepPercent - Calculate percentage step

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getsteppercent-calculate-percentage-step--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getStepPercent/getStepPercent.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getStepPercent - Calculate percentage step'/>

# `getStepPercent`

Calculates what percentage of the total range (between the minimum and maximum values) corresponds to a single unit (or one step = 1). This function is helpful when positioning segments of sliders or progress bars using percentages.

**Parameters:**
- `min: number | undefined` — The minimum value of the range. If `undefined` is passed, `0` is used.
- `max: number` — The maximum value of the range.

**Returns:**
`number` — The percentage (from 0 to 100) that one step occupies. If the maximum value is less than or equal to the minimum, it returns `0`.

```typescript
import { getStepPercent } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Range from 0 to 200, so a step of 1 corresponds to 0.5%
console.log(getStepPercent(0, 200)) // 0.5

// Range from 50 to 150 (difference 100), a step is 1%
console.log(getStepPercent(50, 150)) // 1
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getStepValue - Calculate absolute step value

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-getstepvalue-calculate-absolute-step-value--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/getStepValue/getStepValue.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/getStepValue - Calculate absolute step value'/>

# `getStepValue`

Calculates the absolute value of one percent (`1%`) of the total range (the difference between the minimum and maximum values). This function is useful for determining by how much a slider's or a progress bar's coordinate should be updated when shifted by 1%.

**Parameters:**
- `min: number | undefined` — The minimum value of the range. If `undefined` is passed, `0` is used.
- `max: number` — The maximum value of the range.

**Returns:**
`number` — The value representing 1% of the range's length. If the maximum value is less than or equal to the minimum, it returns `0`.

```typescript
import { getStepValue } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Range from 0 to 500, so 1% corresponds to 5 units
console.log(getStepValue(0, 500)) // 5

// Range from 50 to 150 (difference 100), 1% is 1 unit
console.log(getStepValue(50, 150)) // 1
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/goScroll - Quick scroll change

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-goscroll-quick-scroll-change--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/goScroll/goScroll.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/goScroll - Quick scroll change'/>

# `goScroll`

Quick change of scroll at the element to the required element.

**Parameters:**
- `selector: string` — the selected an element, the scroll position of which needs to be changed.
- `elementTo: HTMLElement | undefined` — the element to which you need to scroll.
- `elementCenter: HTMLElement` — the element that needs to be centered.

```typescript
import { goScroll } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const container = document.querySelector('.container')
const target = document.querySelector('.target')

goScroll('.container', target)
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/goScrollSmooth - Smooth scrolling to element

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-goscrollsmooth-smooth-scrolling-to-element--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/goScrollSmooth/goScrollSmooth.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/goScrollSmooth - Smooth scrolling to element'/>

# `goScrollSmooth`

Provides a smooth scrolling experience navigating the browser window or container to the designated element.

The function first verifies whether the target element supports the native `scrollIntoView` method. If it does, and no `shift` (offset) parameter is given, it relies on the browser's native implementation. Conversely, when a `shift` is required (for instance, to clear a fixed header), the function calculates the offset using `getBoundingClientRect` and delegates the scrolling to `window.scrollTo`.

**Parameters:**
- `element: E` — The target element (`HTMLElement`) to scroll to.
- `options: ScrollIntoViewOptions` — (Optional) Configuration options forwarded to `scrollIntoView` (e.g., `behavior`, `block`, `inline`).
- `shift: number` — (Optional) Top margin offset (in pixels) applied to prevent the targeted element from being obscured by sticky/fixed navigation components. Defaults to `0`.

**Returns:**
The function does not return a value (`void`).

```typescript
import { goScrollSmooth } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const section = document.getElementById('contact-us')

// Standard smooth scroll to element execution
goScrollSmooth(section)

// Scroll resolving a 60px shift (safeguarding past a fixed header)
goScrollSmooth(section, { behavior: 'smooth' }, 60)
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/goScrollTo - Scroll container to element

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-goscrollto-scroll-container-to-element--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/goScrollTo/goScrollTo.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/goScrollTo - Scroll container to element'/>

# `goScrollTo`

Executes scrolling within a defined container element to ensure that the designated child target element falls into the visible area. The function retrieves the bounding client rects (`getBoundingClientRect`) of both elements and engages the `scrollBy` method based on relative coordinate offsets.

Unlike `goScrollSmooth` (which scrolls the general window or delegates to `scrollIntoView`), `goScrollTo` operates through deliberate margins calculating exact inner distances within an explicitly provided container framework.

**Parameters:**
- `element: HTMLElement | undefined` — The parent scroll container element.
- `elementTo: HTMLElement | undefined` — The targeted goal element (normally nestled within the container parent) needing focus.
- `behavior: ScrollBehavior` — (Optional) Adjusts the scroll animation style, defers to `'smooth'` automatically.

**Returns:**
Commits the side-effect scroll adjustment safely returning `void`.

```typescript
import { goScrollTo } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const list = document.getElementById('item-list')
const targetItem = document.getElementById('item-42')

// Safely animates scrolling across the list down to item 42
goScrollTo(list, targetItem)
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/handleShare - Share data via Web Share API

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-handleshare-share-data-via-web-share-api--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/handleShare/handleShare.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/handleShare - Share data via Web Share API'/>

# `handleShare`

Invokes the native sharing mechanism of the device as part of the Web Share API.
Check support with `isShare` before calling.

**Parameters:**
- `data: ShareData` — an object containing data to share (title, text, url).

**Returns:**
`Promise<boolean>` — `true` if the data was shared successfully, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { handleShare, isShare } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const shareData = {
  title: 'Check out this website!',
  text: 'I found something interesting here.',
  url: 'https://example.com'
}

if (isShare()) {
  const success = await handleShare(shareData)
  
  if (success) {
    console.log('Shared successfully!')
  }
}
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/inArray - Check if value is in array

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-inarray-check-if-value-is-in-array--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/inArray/inArray.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/inArray - Check if value is in array'/>

# `inArray`

Checks if the value is in the current array.

**Parameters:**
- `array: T[]` — array for checking.
- `value: T` — value to be checked.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the value is in the array, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { inArray } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const array = [1, 2, 3]
console.log(inArray(array, 2)) // true
console.log(inArray(array, 4)) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/initScrollbarOffset - Initialize scrollbar offset

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-initscrollbaroffset-initialize-scrollbar-offset--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/initScrollbarOffset/initScrollbarOffset.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/initScrollbarOffset - Initialize scrollbar offset'/>

# `initScrollbarOffset`

An asynchronous utility configured to calculate the underlying system width of the browser's scrollbar, safely rendering the result (in pixels) securely via the globally attached CSS custom variable named `--sys-scrollbar-offset` appended dynamically onto `<body>`.

Dedicating precise numbers for system scrollbar sizing is an imperative design layer for constructing elegant modal overlays, flyouts, and body-scrolling locks without the page layout aggressively jumping back and forth. Resolving this size limits layout shift jitter.

The function closely synchronizes with the `ScrollbarWidth` logic mapping internal to this package for the execution heavy-lifting.


**Returns:**
An asynchronous callback evaluating to `Promise<void>`, invoking the side-effect DOM-var injections exclusively.

```typescript
import { initScrollbarOffset } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Triggers sizing capture on app startup
await initScrollbarOffset()

// Utilize styling later implicitly:
// body[data-scroll-locked] { padding-right: var(--sys-scrollbar-offset, 0px); }
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/intersectKey - Intersect objects by keys

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-intersectkey-intersect-objects-by-keys--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/intersectKey/intersectKey.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/intersectKey - Intersect objects by keys'/>

# `intersectKey`

Analyzes two objects (or arrays) and returns a new object containing only the properties from the first object whose keys also exist in the second object. The values for the resulting object are extracted from the primary object (`data`).

This function is highly useful for filtering payload data against an allowed schema footprint or synchronizing partial models.

**Parameters:**
- `data: T` — The primary source object that supplies the final values.
- `comparison: C` — The reference object whose keys are used strictly for existence checks.

**Returns:**
`Record<KT & KC, T[KT]>` — A newly constructed object containing only intersecting properties. If arguments are missing or not objects, it returns an empty object `{}`.

```typescript
import { intersectKey } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const inputData = { id: 1, name: 'John', age: 30, _internalToken: 'xyz' }
const allowedSchema = { id: null, name: null, role: null }

// Returns only the keys from inputData that are present in allowedSchema
const result = intersectKey(inputData, allowedSchema)
console.log(result) // { id: 1, name: 'John' }
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isApiSuccess - Check if API response is successful

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isapisuccess-check-if-api-response-is-successful--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isApiSuccess/isApiSuccess.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isApiSuccess - Check if API response is successful'/>

# `isApiSuccess`

Checks if the API response is successful.

The check is performed according to the following rules:
- If the data is an array, it is considered a success.
- If the data is an object, the success is determined by the `status === 'success'` or `success === true` fields.
- If the `statusObject` field is present, the success is determined by the HTTP status code (starts with 2).
- If none of the fields are present, the success is determined by the global API status.

**Parameters:**
- `data: ApiData<T>` — API response data.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the response is successful, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { isApiSuccess } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isApiSuccess({ status: 'success' })) // true
console.log(isApiSuccess({ success: true })) // true
console.log(isApiSuccess([])) // true
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isArray - Check if value is an array

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isarray-check-if-value-is-an-array--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isArray/isArray.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isArray - Check if value is an array'/>

# `isArray`

Checks if the values are arrays.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — input value.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the value is an array, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { isArray } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isArray([1, 2, 3])) // true
console.log(isArray({})) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isDifferent - Check for object differences

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isdifferent-check-for-object-differences--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isDifferent/isDifferent.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isDifferent - Check for object differences'/>

# `isDifferent`

Performs an assessment to determine if two provided objects differ from each other. 

The function operates by initially comparing the total number of keys (properties) in each object. If those values match, it proceeds to execute a rigid value comparison of those keys through the internal `forEach` utility loop.

**Parameters:**
- `value: ObjectItem<T>` — The current (new) object or data entity being evaluated.
- `old: ObjectItem<T>` — The previous (old) reference object to compare against.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Returns `true` if there are any differences detected between the objects (either by property count mismatch or strict value inequality), or `false` if they are entirely identical.

```typescript
import { isDifferent } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const oldState = { id: 1, active: false }
const newState = { id: 1, active: true }

// Objects differ in the `active` property
console.log(isDifferent(newState, oldState)) // true

const sameState = { id: 1, active: false }

// Objects are completely identical
console.log(isDifferent(sameState, oldState)) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isDomData - Check environment for Data URL

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isdomdata-check-environment-for-data-url--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isDomData/isDomData.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isDomData - Check environment for Data URL'/>

# `isDomData`

A straightforward utility that verifies whether the current execution context operates within a location (`location.href`) prefixed by the `data:` schema constraint. This proves highly useful for isolating environments running inline scripts via data-URIs or rendering distinct sandboxes (like specific `iframes`).

**Parameters:**
This function does not accept any arguments.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Returns `true` if the global document's URL originates from `data:`, yielding `false` in any other scenario.

```typescript
import { isDomData } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Executing code natively on 'https://example.com'
console.log(isDomData()) // false

// Executing script bundled inside a 'data:text/html,...' instance
console.log(isDomData()) // true
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isDomRuntime - Check for browser runtime

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isdomruntime-check-for-browser-runtime--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isDomRuntime/isDomRuntime.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isDomRuntime - Check for browser runtime'/>

# `isDomRuntime`

Determines whether the current code is being executed within a browser environment.

This check asserts the availability of the global `window` object alongside its `window.document` property. Utilizing this utility is a standard approach to guarantee that DOM-specific API invocations (like scroll adjustments, event listeners, or element querying) will not trigger crash errors during Server-Side Rendering (SSR) phases or within isolated Node.js environments.

**Parameters:**
This functionality operates without requiring any defined parameters.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Returns `true` if the code runs in the browser (where `window` and `document` are present). It produces `false` if executed on the server side (such as Node.js instances or static generation builds).

```typescript
import { isDomRuntime } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

if (isDomRuntime()) {
  // Safely interface with window.localStorage or the DOM
  window.localStorage.setItem('visited', 'yes')
  document.body.classList.add('client-rendered')
} else {
  // Manage server-side (SSR) routing and data logic
  console.log('Rendering on server...')
}
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isElementVisible - Check if element is visible

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-iselementvisible-check-if-element-is-visible--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isElementVisible/isElementVisible.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isElementVisible - Check if element is visible'/>

# `isElementVisible`

Checks if an element is visible (not hidden by CSS and is in the DOM).
An element can be off-screen and still be considered visible.

**Parameters:**
- `elementSelectors: ElementOrString<E>` — selectors for matching or an Element.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the element is visible, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { isElementVisible } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const div = document.createElement('div')
document.body.appendChild(div)
console.log(isElementVisible(div)) // true

div.style.display = 'none'
console.log(isElementVisible(div)) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isEnter - Check for Enter or Space key press

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isenter-check-for-enter-or-space-key-press--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isEnter/isEnter.en.mdx
import {Meta} from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isEnter - Check for Enter or Space key press'/>

# `isEnter`

Checks if the pressed key is `Enter` or `Space`. This function is useful for handling keyboard events when you need to react to action confirmation or element activation, while supporting accessibility (a11y) and older browsers.

**Parameters:**
- `event: KeyboardEvent` — the keyboard event object.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the `Enter` or `Space` key is pressed, otherwise `false`.

**Supported event properties:**
The function checks the following event properties for maximum compatibility:
- `code`: `'Enter'`, `'Space'`
- `key`: `'Enter'`, `' '`, `'Spacebar'`
- `keyCode`: `13`, `32`

```typescript
import { isEnter } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const onKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
  if (isEnter(event)) {
    console.log('Enter or Space pressed')
    // Perform action
  }
}
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isFilled - Check if a value is filled

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isfilled-check-if-a-value-is-filled--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isFilled/isFilled.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isFilled - Check if a value is filled'/>

# `isFilled`

A versatile utility for confirming whether a given variable is meaningfully "filled" with data. This function intelligently inspects an array of types including numbers, strings, booleans, arrays, and standard objects to evaluate emptiness beyond basic JavaScript falsiness.

Unlike the generic truthy check (`if (value)`), `isFilled`:
- Resolves arrays as empty (`false`) if their `.length` equals 0.
- Assesses objects as empty (`false`) when all of their mapped values strictly result to `null`.
- Flags definitive string placeholders like `'undefined'`, `'null'`, `'false'`, `'[]'` as entirely empty.
- Out of the box, treats the string `'0'` as empty, although this is modularly configurable via the secondary `zeroTrue` prop.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — The targeted input value of any type anticipating validation.
- `zeroTrue: boolean` — (Optional) When flagged as `true`, interprets the explicit string `'0'` as meaningfully filled (returns `true`), avoiding its traditional falsy deduction.

**Returns:**
`boolean` (`value is Exclude<T, EmptyValue>`) — Emits `true` if the variable is populated, or `false` when empty. This acts as a TypeScript type guard narrowing out `EmptyValue` definitions.

```typescript
import { isFilled } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isFilled('Some text')) // true
console.log(isFilled(100)) // true
console.log(isFilled([1, 2, 3])) // true
console.log(isFilled({ data: 'ok' })) // true

// Scenarios evaluated as empty
console.log(isFilled('')) // false
console.log(isFilled([])) // false
console.log(isFilled({ a: null })) // false
console.log(isFilled('null')) // false
console.log(isFilled('0')) // false

// Applying zeroTrue override
console.log(isFilled('0', true)) // true
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isFloat - Check if value is a number or float

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isfloat-check-if-value-is-a-number-or-float--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isFloat/isFloat.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isFloat - Check if value is a number or float'/>

# `isFloat`

Checks if the value is an integer or a floating-point number.

**Parameters:**
- `value: any` — values for checking.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the value is a number or a string representation of a number, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { isFloat } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isFloat(123)) // true
console.log(isFloat('123.45')) // true
console.log(isFloat('abc')) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isFunction - Check if value is a function

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isfunction-check-if-value-is-a-function--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isFunction/isFunction.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isFunction - Check if value is a function'/>

# `isFunction`

Determines whether the provided value is an executable function (including arrow functions, asynchronous functions, and generator functions).

The check operates primarily via the `instanceof Function` operator, with a fallback safety check against `typeof callback === 'function'`.

**Parameters:**
- `callback: T` — The variable to be tested.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Yields `true` when the argument is a valid function, returning `false` otherwise. Functions as a dedicated TypeScript type guard narrowing the type specifically down to `FunctionArgs<any, any>`.

```typescript
import { isFunction } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const myLogger = () => console.log('Hi!')

console.log(isFunction(myLogger)) // true
console.log(isFunction({ myLogger })) // false
console.log(isFunction('myLogger')) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isInDom - Check if element is in DOM

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isindom-check-if-element-is-in-dom--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isInDom/isInDom.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isInDom - Check if element is in DOM'/>

# `isInDom`

Checks if an element is still in the DOM tree.

**Parameters:**
- `element: ElementOrString<E>` — selectors for matching or an Element.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the element is in the DOM, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { isInDom } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const div = document.createElement('div')
document.body.appendChild(div)
console.log(isInDom(div)) // true
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isInput - Check for input element

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isinput-check-for-input-element--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isInput/isInput.en.mdx
import {Meta} from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isInput - Check for input element'/>

# `isInput`

Checks if the element is an input field (`input`, `textarea`, `select`) or an element with the `contenteditable` attribute.

**Parameters:**
- `element: HTMLElement | EventTarget | null` — the element to check.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the element is an input field or editable, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { isInput } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const input = document.createElement('input')
isInput(input) // true

const div = document.createElement('div')
isInput(div) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isIntegerBetween - Check value within integer bounds

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isintegerbetween-check-value-within-integer-bounds--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isIntegerBetween/isIntegerBetween.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isIntegerBetween - Check value within integer bounds'/>

# `isIntegerBetween`

Inspects whether a targeted float or integer actively rests within a specifically demarcated integer interval bounds.

The threshold is established by flooring the foundational `between` input parameter. The function secures a true match exactly when the checking measurement is comprehensively greater than or equal to the grounded lower boundary, whilst being strictly inferior to the successive integer step.

Formula representation: `floor(between) <= value < floor(between) + 1`

**Parameters:**
- `value: number` — The comparative number undergoing the test.
- `between: number` — The base origin dictating the interval bounds via mathematical floor rounding.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Evaluates to `true` whenever the measurement comfortably exists within the outlined mathematical floor span.

```typescript
import { isIntegerBetween } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Formed Interval: [5, 6)
console.log(isIntegerBetween(5.2, 5.8)) // true (Math.floor(5.8) = 5)
console.log(isIntegerBetween(5.99, 5))  // true
console.log(isIntegerBetween(6, 5))     // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isNull - Check for null or undefined

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isnull-check-for-null-or-undefined--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isNull/isNull.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isNull - Check for null or undefined'/>

# `isNull`

An explicit, stringent utility focused entirely on confirming if a presented variable strictly equals either `null` or `undefined`.

Distinguished from a conventional negative falsy check like `!value` (which intercepts valid zeroes `0`, vacuous strings `''`, and `false` toggles), this function deliberately sidesteps those values, honing in flawlessly on absent definitions.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — Any inclusive subject variable requested to be verified.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Returns `true` uniformly whenever confronted with a raw `null` or an isolated `undefined` marker. It likewise fulfills the responsibility as a TypeScript type guard, securely minimizing the scope to `Undefined` (`null | undefined`).

```typescript
import { isNull } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isNull(null)) // true
console.log(isNull(undefined)) // true

// Routine falsy traps revert as false logically
console.log(isNull(0)) // false
console.log(isNull('')) // false
console.log(isNull(false)) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isNumber - Check if value is a number

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isnumber-check-if-value-is-a-number--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isNumber/isNumber.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isNumber - Check if value is a number'/>

# `isNumber`

A versatile utility engineered to ascertain if an incoming argument acts effectively as a valid numerical entity.

This evaluation effortlessly handles the native numeric typing (`typeof value === 'number'`), meaning it natively clears primitive numbers (inclusive of unique types like `NaN`). Simultaneously, it expands validation to test strings by running a robust RegEx check ensuring that the entirety of the string constitutes a valid numeric format (including negative values and fractional decimals).

**Parameters:**
- `value: any` — The arbitrary data item to screen.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Emits `true` if the subject naturally belongs to the quantitative number type or if it's a completely digital string structure. Displays `false` in every alternative state.

```typescript
import { isNumber } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isNumber(150)) // true
console.log(isNumber(3.14)) // true
console.log(isNumber('720')) // true (Numeric String allowed natively)

console.log(isNumber('-50')) // true (Negative numbers are supported)
console.log(isNumber('3.14')) // true (Decimals are allowed too)
console.log(isNumber('15a')) // false (Fails due to alphanumeric content)
console.log(isNumber(null)) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isObject - Check if value is an object

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isobject-check-if-value-is-an-object--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isObject/isObject.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isObject - Check if value is an object'/>

# `isObject`

A foundational utility explicitly formulated for performing safe, immediate checks regarding whether a given argument legitimately constitutes an object.

In pure JavaScript logic, `typeof null` fundamentally yields the notorious `'object'` string result, inadvertently instigating phantom bugs. `isObject` expertly navigates around this notorious quirk by initiating a truthy checkpoint (`Boolean(value)`) completely shielding execution paths from `null` or undefined pitfalls prior to affirming the exact object type condition. Take caution to realize that structural arrays (`[]`) count as standard objects under JS protocols, so they successfully yield `true` here.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — Any inclusive subject evaluation target variable.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Yields `true` solely if the entity demonstrates fundamental object classification (including collections like arrays). Defaults to generating `false` outputs across null bindings, isolated variables, primitives, and actionable functions. Acts simultaneously as a strict TypeGuard reducing typings to `Record<any, any>`.

```typescript
import { isObject } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isObject({ id: 1 })) // true
console.log(isObject([1, 2, 3])) // true (Arrays are strictly typed as objects underlying JS!)

// Safeguarded boundaries rejecting familiar JS snags
console.log(isObject(null)) // false
console.log(isObject(100)) // false
console.log(isObject('string')) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isObjectNotArray - Strict object verification

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isobjectnotarray-strict-object-verification--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isObjectNotArray/isObjectNotArray.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isObjectNotArray - Strict object verification'/>

# `isObjectNotArray`

Executes an advanced screening operation directly ensuring the tested item constitutes a textbook plain object structure `{}` (Record) while being **assuredly devoid of any array traits**.

Following the intrinsic principle that the elementary `isObject` utility registers `true` inherently when probing arrays (due to JavaScript's rudimentary heritage), this precise function rectifies that quirk. By fusing foundational `isObject(value)` authentication mechanics with a robust `!Array.isArray(value)` safeguard mechanism, arrays are systematically disqualified.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — Any element waiting for inspection.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Generates `true` solely corresponding to authentic objects structured as `{ key: 'value' }`. Yields `false` automatically when combating arrays `[]`, elusive nulls, functional instances, or standard primitive values. Employs reliable type guarding capabilities that carefully extract the pure Object Record architecture while discarding intersecting null, undefined or array overlaps.

```typescript
import { isObjectNotArray } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Successful validation boundaries
console.log(isObjectNotArray({ id: 1, name: 'Test' })) // true

// Scenarios safely bypassed without tripping false positives
console.log(isObjectNotArray([1, 2, 3])) // false (Array defense shield actively rejects arrays!)
console.log(isObjectNotArray(null)) // false
console.log(isObjectNotArray('string')) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isSelected - Check if a value is selected

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isselected-check-if-a-value-is-selected--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isSelected/isSelected.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isSelected - Check if a value is selected'/>

# `isSelected`

A multi-purpose utility dedicated to verifying if a particular reference value matches the desired selection criteria. This function accommodates multiple dynamic comparison strategies based heavily on the architectural structure of the `selected` argument:

1. **Prohibit Nuances:** If the incoming test variable (`value`) manifests as `null` or `undefined`, the function safely terminates with a flat `false`.
2. **Array Validations:** In instances where the `selected` payload arrives configured as an array `[]`, the algorithm utilizes `.includes(value)` to search the array for a direct hit.
3. **Smart Numeric Casting:** If both target variables theoretically align as numbers (determined via the `isNumber()` sub-function), the engine actively homogenizes them parsing them strictly to digit comparisons irrespective of their exact string origins (e.g., `'5'` translates safely into matching pure integer `5`).
4. **Failsafe Comparison:** Absent any of the conditions outlined above, it retreats to standard strict equality `value === selected` matching.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — The active element or context payload waiting for confirmation.
- `selected: T | T[] | S` — The benchmark reference acting as the target check marker; this could seamlessly be a sole primitive or a broader array array list.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Renders `true` assuming the initial argument perfectly corresponds to the requested target data boundary, projecting `false` otherwise.

```typescript
import { isSelected } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Standard discrete checks
console.log(isSelected('apple', 'apple')) // true
console.log(isSelected('apple', 'banana')) // false

// Validating inclusion against a data array collection
console.log(isSelected(2, [1, 2, 3])) // true
console.log(isSelected('dog', ['cat', 'mouse'])) // false

// Intelligent Cross-type Casted Number matching (string against raw ints)
console.log(isSelected('5', 5)) // true

// Safety checks
console.log(isSelected(null, 1)) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isSelectedByList - Check selection across a list

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isselectedbylist-check-selection-across-a-list--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isSelectedByList/isSelectedByList.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isSelectedByList - Check selection across a list'/>

# `isSelectedByList`

An advanced utility explicitly extending the boundaries of the elementary `isSelected` functionality. It serves precisely to evaluate whether **all underlying elements** within a submitted list array (`values`) effectively align with the outlined verification goal (`selected`).

If presented with a solitary value instead of an array collection, the program seamlessly delegates the heavy routing straight back to `isSelected`. Yet, if a mapped array array enters the fray (`values`), the software executes a holistic `.every()` iterator technique to solidly assure that **each discrete component** independently triumphs over the isolated `isSelected(item, selected)` test parameter.

**Parameters:**
- `values: T | T[]` — The single payload or a bundled array array collection mandating cross-checking.
- `selected: T | T[]` — The target anchor point criteria (could be a string, numeral, or a mapped payload collection representing allowed terms).

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Commits to returning `true` exclusively when completely all distributed variables simultaneously conquer the testing perimeter hurdle.

```typescript
import { isSelectedByList } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Blanket success spanning the full bulk mapped uniformly against one specific key
console.log(isSelectedByList(['apple', 'apple'], 'apple')) // true

// Overwhelming validation granted since every item from values nests safely inside the selected pool bounds
console.log(isSelectedByList(['apple', 'cherry'], ['banana', 'apple', 'cherry'])) // true

// Failure detected. 'date' is absent from the permissions block (selected array). Outputs false through .every() mechanics. 
console.log(isSelectedByList(['apple', 'date'], ['banana', 'apple', 'cherry'])) // false

// Upon dropping a single index, inherently collapses into acting purely like isSelected
console.log(isSelectedByList('apple', 'apple')) // true
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isShare - Check for Web Share API support

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isshare-check-for-web-share-api-support--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isShare/isShare.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isShare - Check for Web Share API support'/>

# `isShare`

Checks if the Web Share API is supported in the current environment.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — `true` if the Web Share API is supported, otherwise `false`.

```typescript
import { isShare } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

if (isShare()) {
  console.log('Sharing is supported!')
}
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isString - Check if value is a string

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-isstring-check-if-value-is-a-string--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isString/isString.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isString - Check if value is a string'/>

# `isString`

A fundamental yet highly reliable utility to assert whether the provided element constitutes a string type.

This resource leans on the standard native evaluation `typeof value === 'string'` to verify the intrinsic nature of the variable. By strictly enforcing this check, it instinctively filters out extraneous data dimensions including numbers, logic booleans, arrays, complex objects, and undefined traces.

**Parameters:**
- `value: T` — The generic input material submitted for type verification.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Emits `true` if the evaluated target inherently functions as a string primitive. Spits out `false` in every opposing scenario. Further establishes itself as a strict TypeScript type guard, proactively confining the subsequent variable type specifically down to `string`.

```typescript
import { isString } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(isString('Hello, World!')) // true
console.log(isString('')) // true (empty strings are inherently valid strings)

// Filtering out dissenting types
console.log(isString(123)) // false
console.log(isString(null)) // false
console.log(isString({})) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isWindow - Check if object is Window

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-iswindow-check-if-object-is-window--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/isWindow/isWindow.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/isWindow - Check if object is Window'/>

# `isWindow`

A precise utility built specifically to authorize whether an inserted payload organically represents the global browser `window` object.

The blueprint relies initially on routing logic through `isDomRuntime()` ensuring smooth validation devoid of crashes if mistakenly fired in disconnected Server-Side Render (SSR) arenas. Once confirmed safe inside a browser space, it triggers a stringent equality checkpoint comparing the payload straightforwardly directly against the overarching global `window` instance.

**Parameters:**
- `element: E` — An active hook, DOM Node, or variable queued up for matching against.

**Returns:**
`boolean` — Translates strictly into `true` only when actively engaged under a browser umbrella and the selected element identically matches the base `window` object. Operates effectively as a Type Guard filtering down boundaries narrowing accurately toward `Window`.

```typescript
import { isWindow } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

// Spot on validation
console.log(isWindow(window)) // true
console.log(isWindow(globalThis.window)) // true

// Conflicting DOM elements returning negated
console.log(isWindow(document)) // false
console.log(isWindow(document.body)) // false
console.log(isWindow({})) // false
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/random - Generate a random number

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-random-generate-a-random-number--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/random/random.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/random - Generate a random number'/>

# `random`

Generates and returns a random integer within a specified range from `min` to `max` inclusive.

**Parameters:**
- `min: number` — The lowest possible value.
- `max: number` — The highest possible value.

**Returns:**
`number` — A random integer.

```typescript
import { random } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(random(1, 10)) // e.g., 7
console.log(random(50, 100)) // e.g., 84
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/removeCommonPrefix - Remove common prefix

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-removecommonprefix-remove-common-prefix--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/removeCommonPrefix/removeCommonPrefix.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/removeCommonPrefix - Remove common prefix'/>

# `removeCommonPrefix`

Removes a common prefix from the beginning of the main string. If the main string starts with the specified prefix, the function trims it and returns the string without leading and trailing whitespaces.

**Parameters:**
- `mainStr: string` — The main string.
- `prefix: string` — The prefix string to remove.

**Returns:**
`string` — A new string without the prefix and trimmed of whitespaces.

```typescript
import { removeCommonPrefix } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

console.log(removeCommonPrefix('Hello, world!', 'Hello, ')) // 'world!'
console.log(removeCommonPrefix('user_name', 'user_')) // 'name'
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/replaceComponentName - Replace component name in text

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-replacecomponentname-replace-component-name-in-text--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/replaceComponentName/replaceComponentName.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/replaceComponentName - Replace component name in text'/>

# `replaceComponentName`

Replaces HTML tags of a specific component within a text string with new ones. It searches for opening (`<name`) and closing (`</name`) tags case-insensitively and replaces them with `componentName`.

**Parameters:**
- `text: string | undefined` — The original text.
- `name: string` — The component name to replace.
- `componentName: string` — The new component name.

**Returns:**
`string | undefined` — The modified text with new tags, or `undefined` if the original text was not provided.

```typescript
import { replaceComponentName } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const template = '<Button>Click me</Button>'
console.log(replaceComponentName(template, 'Button', 'AppButton')) 
// '<AppButton>Click me</AppButton>'
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/replaceRecursive - Recursive merge of objects or arrays

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-replacerecursive-recursive-merge-of-objects-or-arrays--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/replaceRecursive/replaceRecursive.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/replaceRecursive - Recursive merge of objects or arrays'/>

# `replaceRecursive`

Performs a deep (recursive) merge of the target object or array with data from a replacement object/array. If the `isMerge` parameter is enabled, arrays are concatenated with duplicates removed; otherwise, arrays from the replacement overwrite the original ones.

**Parameters:**
- `array: ObjectItem<I>` — The target array or object whose elements will be replaced/merged.
- `replacement?: ObjectOrArray<I>` — Arrays or objects from which elements will be extracted for replacement.
- `isMerge: boolean` — Flag to merge arrays of the same name (defaults to `true`). If `false`, elements are simply overwritten.

**Returns:**
`ObjectItem<I>` — A new object or array that is the result of the merge. The original object is not mutated.

```typescript
import { replaceRecursive } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const target = { a: [1, 2], b: { x: 1 } }
const source = { a: [2, 3], b: { y: 2 } }

console.log(replaceRecursive(target, source)) 
// { a: [1, 2, 3], b: { x: 1, y: 2 } }

console.log(replaceRecursive(target, source, false)) 
// { a: [2, 3], b: { x: 1, y: 2 } }
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/replaceTemplate - Substituting values into a template string

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-replacetemplate-substituting-values-into-a-template-string--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/replaceTemplate/replaceTemplate.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/replaceTemplate - Substituting values into a template string'/>

# `replaceTemplate`

Searches the original string for markers in the form `[pattern]` and replaces them with corresponding values from the provided object. If the marker's value is a function, it is automatically called, and its result is substituted into the string.

**Parameters:**
- `value: string` — The template string containing markers (e.g., `[name]`).
- `replaces: Record<string, string | FunctionReturn<string>>` — An object containing replacement data, where the key is the marker name inside brackets, and the value is a string or a function returning a string.

**Returns:**
`string` — A new string with the substituted values.

```typescript
import { replaceTemplate } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'

const template = 'Hello, [name]! Your balance: [balance].'
const data = {
  name: 'John',
  balance: () => '$100' // The function is evaluated during substitution
}

console.log(replaceTemplate(template, data)) 
// 'Hello, John! Your balance: $100.'
```

@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/resizeImageByMax - Resize image by maximum dimension

dxtmisha-en-functional-basic-functions-resizeimagebymax-resize-image-by-maximum-dimension--docs · ../wiki/src/media/functional/functional-basic/functions/resizeImageByMax/resizeImageByMax.en.mdx
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title='@dxtmisha/en/functional-basic/Functions/resizeImageByMax - Resize image by maximum dimension'/>

# `resizeImageByMax`

Proportionally resizes an image to fit within the specified maximum size constraints (by width, height, or automatically by the longest side). Returns a Base64-encoded Data URL of the scaled image.

This function is useful for compressing images on the fly before uploading to a server.

**Parameters:**
- `image: HTMLImageElement | string` — The image element or URL.
- `maxSize: number` — The maximum allowed size in pixels (for width or height).
- `type: ResizeImageByMaxType` — The scaling strategy: `'auto'`, `'width'`, or `'height'`. Defaults to `'auto'`.
- `typeData?: string` — Optional MIME type for the resulting Data URL (e.g., `'image/jpeg'`). Defaults to `'image/png'`.

**Returns:**
`string | undefined` — A Data URL string containing the resized image, or `undefined` if the image could not be processed or is already smaller than or equal to `maxSize`.

```typescript
import { resizeImageByMax } from '@dxtmisha/functional-basic'
```
